title: A constant flux has zero local flux difference

source.notes: First local lemma in the conservative flux telescoping ladder.

informal.statement: For a constant real-valued flux, the local one-dimensional flux difference flux(i+1) - flux(i) is zero at every index.
assumptions: i is a natural-number grid index
fluxDifference flux i is defined as flux(i+1) - flux(i)
informal.expected_conclusion: The flux difference of the constant flux function is zero.

declarations.name: fluxDifference
declarations.statement: def fluxDifference (flux : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : Real := flux (i + 1) - flux i

declarations.name: fluxDifference_const_zero
declarations.statement: theorem fluxDifference_const_zero (c : Real) (i : Nat) : fluxDifference (fun _ => c) i = 0

tasks.input: Translate the statement that a constant flux has zero local flux difference into Lean 4 using fluxDifference.
tasks.target: theorem fluxDifference_const_zero (c : Real) (i : Nat) : fluxDifference (fun _ => c) i = 0

tasks.input: Given the fluxDifference definition and theorem statement, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof by unfolding fluxDifference and closing with ring.
